plan: We will trim SPY to reduce its concentration below 20%, aligning with capital preservation.
All leveraged ETFs, SOXL and SPAL, will be sold to eliminate high-risk exposure.
QQQI will be increased to 25% to boost income and stability in the portfolio.
ANET will be bought at 5% as a high-quality addition from the scanner's top setups.
CRWD will be increased to 15% to lean into its current uptrend and positive P&L.
These moves target a more defensive posture, reducing overall portfolio risk.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. Expect lower volatility and drawdown, but also more modest growth potential. Risk is reduced by cutting leverage and high concentration.

moves.note: Add strong growth setup from scanner

plan: We will trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate and rebalance the portfolio.
QQQI will be trimmed to 10% to free up capital for growth-oriented opportunities.
SOXL and SPAL will be sold to eliminate underperforming leveraged positions.
ANET will be bought at 8% and DDOG at 7%, adding high-quality growth names from the scanner.
CRWD will be increased to 15% to capitalize on its positive trend and P&L.
This plan aims for risk-first growth, balancing broad exposure with quality tech.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It aims for a blend of stability and growth, with moderate volatility. Drawdowns are expected to be contained but still present.

moves.note: Reduce broad index, shift to focused…

plan: We will trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate and reallocate capital.
QQQI and QQQ will be trimmed to 5% and 10% respectively, shifting focus to high-growth opportunities.
SOXL and SPAL will be sold to exit underperforming leveraged positions and reallocate.
ANET will be bought at 12% and DDOG at 10%, concentrating in top scanner setups.
CRWD will be increased to 20% to lean heavily into its strong uptrend and positive P&L.
This plan aggressively targets growth, using concentration in high-scoring setups.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretch growth. It involves higher concentration and potential for larger drawdowns due to increased exposure to specific names. Leveraged ETFs are avoided due to current underperformance and decay, but concentration in high-beta names still increases risk.

moves.note: Reduce broad index, shift to income

plan: We will trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and reallocate towards income-generating assets.
QQQI will be significantly increased to 35% to maximize monthly distributions.
QQQ will be trimmed to 5% to further reduce growth-oriented broad index exposure.
SMH, SOXL, and SPAL will be sold to eliminate high-beta sector and leveraged ETF exposure.
CRWD will be trimmed to 5% to reduce high-beta single name risk.
This plan prioritizes parking cash for yield and achieving steady monthly distributions.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, focusing on steady distributions and capital preservation. Expect lower growth potential and reduced volatility, but NAV can still decline in market selloffs. Distributions are variable and not guaranteed.
